message: Helper not available for this program type
notes: cause: Each BPF program type has an allowlist of helpers; the verifier rejects calls outside that allowlist. The offending helper is bpf_probe_read_user. The most common cause is calling a tracing-only helper from a network program or vice versa.

0: R1=ctx() R10=fp0
; bpf.probe.read_user(buf, sizeof(buf), addr);
1: (85) call bpf_probe_read_user#112
unknown func bpf_probe_read_user
suggest: Use only Horizon compiler-known helpers for this program kind. If you need a helper that is technically available but not yet wrapped, add a typed helper wrapper to the Horizon stdlib before calling it from .hzn so the validator catches the kind mismatch up front.
